summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting covering road salt appropriations, equipment purchases, zoning resolution amendments, a strategic plan presentation, and several community-related items. Key actions included increasing salt appropriations by $20,000, purchasing a portable washer/steamer for $14,110.65, adopting three zoning text amendments (PDM mixed-use minimum, accessory structure cap removal, and PUD lot size increase), accepting donated AEDs, and presenting the township's first five-year strategic plan. Two part-time firefighter EMTs were hired following an executive session.
decisions: Approved payment of bills for February 11-24, 2026
Approved Chief Myers to attend NFFF CAST team meeting in Baltimore, MD March 8-10
Approved increasing appropriations for salt (MBL contracted services) by $20,000
Approved purchase of Enzo's 4000 PSI Portable Washer Steamer with undercarriage wash unit for $14,110.65 shared by Service and Fire departments
Adopted Resolution R007-2026 to amend Article 14 PDM District Section 1404 to establish minimum 20% non-residential gross floor area
Adopted Resolution R008-2026 (amended title) to amend Article 7 Section 7.01 to remove 2,000 sq ft maximum accessory structure cap
Adopted Resolution R009-2026 (amended title) to increase minimum PUD residential lot size from 5,000 to 10,800 sq ft with setback adjustments
Accepted donation of 91 $5 gift cards for staff from Snack Pack 4L Foundation
Approved sending letter of support to Rep. Schmidt for Historical Society's Anchorage House HVAC state funding request
Approved $250 sponsorship of Goshen Derby event for Goshen Township Historical Society
Accepted donation of three AEDs from Project Heart Start
Adopted Resolution R010-2026 declaring three Phillips Park Stop AED units as obsolete and donating them to American Regulators USA
Entered executive session at 6:57 PM per ORC 121.22G1
Hired part-time firefighter EMT Jesse Blevins at $18/hour effective March 8, 2026
Hired part-time firefighter EMT Ethan Geiger at $18/hour effective March 8, 2026
action_items: Administrator Rose to finalize strategic plan for adoption at next meeting, incorporating Trustee Allen's feedback
Director Langenheim to coordinate Land Use Plan survey public session on March 3 at 6:30 PM downstairs
Administrator Rose to work with Congress marketing team to draft flyer for fire station ceremony (March 8) and open house (March 9)
Chief Rose and Administrator Rose to schedule meeting with Historical Society to discuss HVAC funding and grant collaboration for Anchorage
Medic unit assessment at Horton to be completed by end of next week regarding locked motor, bent frame, and cab evaluation
Trustee Hodges to sign letter of support to Rep. Schmidt on behalf of Board
Director Meadows to purchase 200 tons of salt with the $20,000 appropriation
key_amounts: $20,000 increase in appropriations for road salt (MBL contracted services SALT 2011-330-360-0000)
$14,110.65 for Enzo's 4000 PSI Portable Washer Steamer with undercarriage wash unit
$250 sponsorship for Goshen Township Historical Society Goshen Derby event (table for 6)
91 x $5 gift cards donated by Snack Pack 4L Foundation for township staff
$18/hour rate for two new part-time firefighter EMTs
Approximately 300 tons of salt used this winter, 70 tons remaining
notable_topics: Road salt appropriations increase due to harsh winter
Industrial site cleanup on State Route 28 nearing completion
Medic unit with locked motor and bent frame under evaluation at Horton
Portable washer/steamer purchase shared between Service and Fire departments
Three zoning text amendments: PDM mixed-use 20% non-residential minimum, removal of 2,000 sq ft accessory structure cap, PUD lot size increase from 5,000 to 10,800 sq ft
Goshen Township Historical Society Anchorage House HVAC replacement funding request
Land Use Plan survey and public meeting scheduled March 3
Five-year Strategic Plan presentation (2026-2030) covering flock cameras, levy reviews, SR28 improvements, cemetery expansion, fire station improvements
American 250 flag presentation recognizing Revolutionary War veterans buried in Goshen Township
Donation of three new AEDs from Project Heart Start and surplus of three old AEDs to American Regulators USA
Community center request from resident Judy Burns
New fire station ceremony March 8 and open house March 9
Executive session for employment matters resulting in two part-time firefighter EMT hires
attendees: Jim Poe (Goshen Township Historical Society, 6917 Shiloh Road)
Andy (Goshen Township Historical Society, American 250 flag presentation)
notes: Trustee Allen was absent due to illness. The transcript contains significant portions of garbled/corrupted text likely due to automated transcription errors. The strategic plan will be formally adopted at the next meeting. The meeting included an executive session from 6:57 PM to 7:24 PM. The American 250 flag presentation recognized Revolutionary War veterans who settled in Goshen Township including Ludwig F. Freiberger, Daniel R. Raudenbusch, Johann Jacob Stroop Sr., Louis Freiberger, Jacob Reeder, A. Simmons, and John Irwin. Drew DeMarche from the county will facilitate the March 3 land use plan survey meeting.
title: BOT 2-24-26
